title: Kafka Event Envelope
description: Contract 5 — Every Kafka event produced by any CypherX service MUST use this envelope. The `payload` is event-type-specific (see contracts/kafka/events/*.schema.json). Forward-compatible: unknown top-level fields are tolerated (additionalProperties:true) so producers on a newer envelope version do not break older consumers.

event_id.description: Globally unique identifier of this event (UUID v4). Used for idempotent consumer de-duplication.

event_type.description: Fully-qualified event type, e.g. `cypherx.agent.task.completed`. Mirrors the topic naming convention `cypherx.<domain>.<entity>.<event-type>`. See contracts/kafka/topics.md.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the payload schema for this event_type, e.g. `1.0.0`. Consumers use this to select the correct payload decoder.

produced_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp (millisecond precision) at which the producer emitted this event.

trace_id.description: Distributed-tracing correlation id (UUID) propagated across services. Optional but strongly recommended on every event (Contract 8).

tenant_id.description: Isolation-boundary UUID (Contract 13). Present on every tenant-scoped event. For platform-owned events this is the well-known platform tenant UUID.

producer_service.description: Logical name of the service that produced this event, e.g. `xagent`, `llms-gateway`, `auth`.

producer_version.description: Semantic version of the producing service build, e.g. `1.0.0`. Optional; aids debugging of producer-specific regressions.

partition_key.description: Kafka partition key. MUST default to `tenant_id` for tenant-scoped events to guarantee per-tenant ordering. Event types needing stronger ordering (e.g. per-agent) may override with `agent_id`, but MUST document the override in contracts/kafka/topics.md.

payload.description: Event-type-specific body. Validated against the matching schema under contracts/kafka/events/. Forward-compatible: tolerates unknown fields.
